capture.cameraPose: Desktop browser viewport capture after moving the Input Evidence panel forward/lower and moving the wall clock above the panel sightlines
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP evaluate_script reload to ?visualQa=panel-depth-clock-final-20260505; wait for runtime evidence; take_screenshot fullPage

notes: The ED bay still reads as a coarse runtime preview with primitive actors and sparse equipment; this slice only improves spatial text placement.
Actors remain capsule/head placeholders with no realistic hands, clothing, facial expression, pain response, rigging, or medical-equipment density.
The Simulated EHR, Live Dialogue, and Input Evidence canvas-text panels are readable in the desktop capture, with the Input Evidence title no longer clipped by the dialogue panel or wall clock.
Trace buttons and the Full VR entry affordance remain visible, but this desktop screenshot still cannot prove headset entry, controller select, hand tracking, locomotion, or latency.
The Input Evidence panel has moved out of the central patient and nurse silhouettes from this camera pose, and the wall clock has been raised above the text panel lane.
This is desktop Chrome DevTools visual iteration evidence only; it is not physical Quest proof, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, and not production runtime readiness.
